# One-off — NEW YORK-PRESBYTERIAN HOSPITAL (NY)

```
**To:** Group SVP, CFO, New York-Presbyterian Hospital
**CC:** Chief Security Officer

**Subject:** Ch. 618/2025 compliance — Detect-to-Alert infrastructure for ED and behavioral units

New York's Ch. 618/2025 mandate takes effect January 2027. Your 2,262-bed system — with a high-DSH safety-net profile — faces material compliance risk on gunshot detection, staff panic alerting, and incident location precision across your ED and behavioral health wings.

Firefly's Lattice hardware + Ember software stack handles the Detect→Locate→Alert→Respond loop without IT dependency — critical for systems your scale. We've built for acute care environments with comparable acuity and throughput.

Would a 15-minute walk-through of how Lattice maps to your facility's architecture help clarify readiness?

— Ryan

---
**Why this opening:** Led with the specific, hard deadline (Ch. 618/2025, effective 2027-01-01) and the concrete risk profile (2,262-bed safety-net acute care system with ED + behavioral unit), which is the most concrete signal in the data and the only regulatory trigger that matters to a CFO-level stakeholder.
```
